# Transcrição: 63. Trait 62 - Great Salespeople take the time to practice, drill, rehearse and role play.

- **Fonte:** 63. Trait 62 - Great Salespeople take the time to practice, drill, rehearse and role play.
- **Duração:** 01:35
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:35]

Great salespeople take the time to practice, drill, rehearse and role play. And you're like, yeah, but I know Sam, Sam's a great salesperson. He doesn't role play and drill. Then Sam's not great. Sam's just good. Sam's got gifts. Sam's been in business for a long time. And Sam's been doing well and good for Sam. But Sam ain't great. Because you can't show me somebody that's not great. I'm talking great. Think about athletes, politicians. Think about the greatest people that have ever walked on this planet. And I guarantee you, somewhere along the way, they role played and drilled and practiced and rehearsed. And maybe it was just a lifetime of role playing, drilling, practicing and rehearsing with customers. I don't want to learn like that. I don't want to wait until I'm 90 to finally figure out, oh, I'm good now. I'm finally great. I finally hit it because then I won't have any energy. Look, if you want to be young, vibrant, excited, interested on your game, on your toes, got some life left in you, some vigor, some pep in your step, baby. Role play and drill every day. I can't tell you how important this is. I was 25 years old and I started doing this literally 40 minutes a day, every day. Every day I went to work, I role played on other people, not customers, not phone calls calling in. I role played situations that I wasn't prepared for with somebody that I worked with in order to warm up. In order to get my joint solutes, my mouth working, my head thinking, my head connected to my mouth, my mouth connected to my passion, my heart, role played drill, you'll be great if you do it and you should do it every day.
